turns.q: My order arrived and one item is damaged — what do I do?
turns.replyTail:
Simple Modern Support

Hey there! 🤍 I'm Maddie, Simple Modern's support assistant. I can help with orders, tracking, product questions, and more!

Today
My order arrived and one item is damaged — what do I do?

Hi — I can help with that.

To make sure I give you the right next steps, where was it purchased — simplemodern.com, Amazon, or another retailer?

turns.q: Is a damaged item handled differently from a normal return?
turns.replyTail:
m, not a standard return.
Normal return: this is for unused, non-defective items within 90 days.

For normal returns:

Items must be unused and in original packaging
Return shipping is deducted from the refund
We don’t do direct exchanges
Canadian orders aren’t eligible for returns

For damaged items:

We’ll guide you through a claim instead
Photos are required
The next step depends on where it was purchased

Where did you buy it — simplemodern.com, Amazon, or another retailer?

turns.q: Do you need photos, and where do I send them?
turns.replyTail:
n, or another retailer?

Do you need photos, and where do I send them?

Yes — for an item that arrived damaged, we do need photos.

You can send them either:

here in the chat box, or
by email to support@simplemodern.com

If you have a video, please email it to support@simplemodern.com since video files can be too large for chat.

When you send them, please include your order info so we can help faster.

Where did you purchase it — simplemodern.com, Amazon, or another retailer?

turns.q: Will I get a replacement or a refund?
turns.replyTail:
damaged item is handled as a replacement claim rather than a normal return or refund.

A few important notes:

If it was ordered from us or another retailer, we’ll typically direct you to our claims portal once we have the needed info.
If it was purchased on Amazon and arrived damaged, Amazon would handle the resolution.
I don’t want to promise a refund or replacement until I confirm where it was purchased.

Where did you buy it — simplemodern.com, Amazon, or another retailer?

Do I have to pay anything to return the damaged item?

Not as a standard return.

If an item arrived damaged, it’s usually handled through a damage claim rather than a normal return, so the regular return shipping deduction wouldn’t apply in the same way.

The exact next step depends on where it was purchased. Was it from simplemodern.com, Amazon, or another retailer?

turns.replyTail:
placement take?

It can vary depending on where it was purchased and how the claim is submitted.

I’m not seeing an order under this email, so the next step is to confirm the purchase source first.

In general:

if it was purchased from us or another retailer and arrived damaged, the claim is submitted through our portal
if it was purchased on Amazon and arrived damaged, Amazon handles the replacement timing

turns.q: What if more than one item in my order is affected?
turns.replyTail:
or another retailer?

What if more than one item in my order is affected?

If more than one item arrived damaged, that’s okay — just make sure you include:

photos of each damaged item
which items in the order are affected
your order info

If it was a multi-item order, we’ll need confirmation of exactly which pieces arrived damaged before the claim moves forward.

Where it was purchased still matters for the next step. Was it from simplemodern.com, Amazon, or another retailer?
